key_insight: Geometry-aware operator learning can accurately predict industrial crash dynamics while capturing both deformation and acceleration responses with much lower cost than full finite element simulation.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster crashworthiness design iteration for safety-critical automotive structures, supporting surrogate-based optimization on complex nonlinear transient problems.
method: Use GeoTransolver to learn crash dynamics from complex bumper beam and full-vehicle geometries.
Compare one-shot, time-conditional, and autoregressive temporal prediction recipes.
Add FLARE low-rank attention to cut memory use while preserving long-range geometric interactions.
evidence_signal: The approach is benchmarked on complex bumper beam and full-vehicle crash datasets, with one-shot prediction reported as state-of-the-art and FLARE improving accuracy while reducing memory by about 2x.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and diversity of training crash datasets, and industrial deployment will need validation across unseen geometries, materials, and contact conditions.
paper_title: High-Fidelity Industrial Crash Dynamics Prediction via Geometry-Aware Operator Learning with Memory-Efficient Low-Rank Attention
